## **Problem statement**

Building a capable agent with its own agentic loop is very high effort. For example, anything we build will likely not be as good as Claude Code if we give it the same MCP tools and the same domain-specific skills. Not to mention the various context management and compression capabilities, memory, extensibility, etc.

The goal of Jaeger AI should not be to compete with these 3rd party agentic tools, but to allow using them to best effect. We need a “Bring Your Own Agent” approach.

We want to build a "Bridge" between Jaeger **Go backend**, Jaeger **React frontend**, and a **Claude Code (CC)** agent (or any other compatible agent) using the **A2A protocol**. In this architecture, we are creating a **Control Plane** (the A2A facade) and a **Data Plane** (Jaeger data).

Here is the breakdown of the 2026-standard architecture to make this work.

### ---

**1\. The Component Stack**

To make the Jaeger UI talk to CC via A2A, you need four distinct layers:

| Layer | Component | Implementation |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **User Interface** | **React / AG-UI** | Uses the AG-UI protocol to stream agent thoughts and "Command JSON" to your frontend components. |
| **Agent Facade** | **Go A2A-Server** | A new microservice (or a module in your Jaeger Go backend) that implements the A2A spec (HTTP \+ SSE \+ Agent Cards). |
| **The Harness** | **Claude Agent SDK** | A Node.js sidecar (or Python) that runs the actual CC logic. |
| **The Tools** | **Jaeger MCP Server** | Your existing server that gives CC access to trace data. |

### ---

**2\. The Architecture Diagram**

[https://mermaid.live/edit\#pako:eNp9U2FP2zAQ\_SuWJaSitVmathnJh0ltWlDbARUpmrQGTV5ypBmJXTnOgCH47TsnToEKzV96vrz37t6d-0RjkQD1aSrZbkvW04gTPGX1q0kEeQZcbSLaBKQzkeK-BHkc0ZsGqs\_1fLNgkILEiHwi47Me\_jYMgwKeRPxAezJDXcM7E2TC4juEkc5KZgWTj2QlRQxl-b7U4ifSDCkQEshnMnbG74vp8xqdB6uWgCGZPSjgZSb4fzoLswRiJrG9capdmzvpXOCwrN\_lx63t6UjDlr5jvMOinfF0iV2eh-RUsgLuhbxD3itNH4PdaF4I8g\_Svok0i2\_ew8LpchPkrEqAmMZq6SAgM55mHN7Aa1sH\_o6OcGRFUfEsZgonQE5zcd98Mut9eXnBtTSpyay-Gu9NrvVUf5guW2Hcdq-nbdeLx-EoEYs8opj9Wm-sgenIANFnCyOdRXh50btaBfh0wnB2bHimVkPdG1hvAflz3GACvctKITgQnEOsDTUoPZS6il73WmCFgOV5aWQx-YHkFZQ7wUsgoZLAijdCFgqttxm\_y3iqH1tdSItZhz22w6kp2uFYquyWxapFH07C2o9sCrlibe0GfD2nXVqALFiW4P\_zSRMjqrZQQER9DBO4ZVWu9FN6RiirlAgfeUx9JSvoUimqdEv9W5aXeKt2CVMwzRi-0GKf3TH-Q4iipaRSlzJ0fDcgA1FxRf1R36nB1H-iD9T3htbIcz3XPXFtZziw-136SP1e3-oP3FHfGwyGztAbOqPBc5f-rfVty7P7jmM7rmOPbNc5-fL8Dx\_FUqY](https://mermaid.live/edit#pako:eNp9U2FP2zAQ_SuWJaSitVmathnJh0ltWlDbARUpmrQGTV5ypBmJXTnOgCH47TsnToEKzV96vrz37t6d-0RjkQD1aSrZbkvW04gTPGX1q0kEeQZcbSLaBKQzkeK-BHkc0ZsGqs_1fLNgkILEiHwi47Me_jYMgwKeRPxAezJDXcM7E2TC4juEkc5KZgWTj2QlRQxl-b7U4ifSDCkQEshnMnbG74vp8xqdB6uWgCGZPSjgZSb4fzoLswRiJrG9capdmzvpXOCwrN_lx63t6UjDlr5jvMOinfF0iV2eh-RUsgLuhbxD3itNH4PdaF4I8g_Svok0i2_ew8LpchPkrEqAmMZq6SAgM55mHN7Aa1sH_o6OcGRFUfEsZgonQE5zcd98Mut9eXnBtTSpyay-Gu9NrvVUf5guW2Hcdq-nbdeLx-EoEYs8opj9Wm-sgenIANFnCyOdRXh50btaBfh0wnB2bHimVkPdG1hvAflz3GACvctKITgQnEOsDTUoPZS6il73WmCFgOV5aWQx-YHkFZQ7wUsgoZLAijdCFgqttxm_y3iqH1tdSItZhz22w6kp2uFYquyWxapFH07C2o9sCrlibe0GfD2nXVqALFiW4P_zSRMjqrZQQER9DBO4ZVWu9FN6RiirlAgfeUx9JSvoUimqdEv9W5aXeKt2CVMwzRi-0GKf3TH-Q4iipaRSlzJ0fDcgA1FxRf1R36nB1H-iD9T3htbIcz3XPXFtZziw-136SP1e3-oP3FHfGwyGztAbOqPBc5f-rfVty7P7jmM7rmOPbNc5-fL8Dx_FUqY) 

| `graph TD    subgraph Client["Client (Browser)"]        UI[Jaeger UI + AG-UI Client]    end     subgraph BE["Jaeger Go Backend (Primary Process)"]        J_BE[Jaeger Core / A2A Client]               MCP[Jaeger MCP Extension]    end     subgraph Sidecar["Agent Sidecar (Node.js Process)"]        subgraph "A2A Wrapper (ADK / MS Framework)"            Wrapper[A2A Server Logic]            SDK[Claude Agent SDK / CC Engine]        end    end     %% Communication Flow    Client ~~~ BE    BE ~~~ Sidecar    Wrapper ~~~ SDK     UI -- "AG-UI Protocol" --> J_BE    J_BE -- "A2A Protocol (JSON-RPC + SSE)" --> Wrapper       %% The "Inside-Out" Connection    SDK -- "MCP Tool Calls" --> MCP       %% The Response Stream    SDK -. "Thinking / Actions" .-> Wrapper    Wrapper -. "A2A Artifacts" .-> J_BE    J_BE -. "AG-UI Delta Stream" .-> UI`  |
| :---- |

![][image1]

### 

### **3\. Step-by-Step Execution Flow**

#### **Step A: Discovery (The "Agent Card")**

Your Go backend must host a /.well-known/agent.json file. This tells the UI (and other agents) what this agent is capable of.

{  
  "name": "Jaeger Diagnostic Copilot",  
  "description": "Expert in distributed tracing and root cause analysis.",  
  "endpoint": "https://jaeger.internal/a2a",  
  "skills": \["trace\_analysis", "bottleneck\_detection"\],  
  "mcpServers": \["https://jaeger-mcp.internal"\]  
}

#### **Step B: The "A2A Bridge" (In Go)**

Since CC is a Node.js/Python SDK, but your backend is Go, your Go server acts as a **Proxy**.

1. **Incoming:** The React UI sends a query: "Why is trace 882f slow?"  
2. **Orchestration:** The Go backend receives the A2A request, validates the user's session, and forwards a **Task** to the Claude Agent SDK sidecar.  
3. **Handoff:** The Go server uses a gRPC or HTTP stream to communicate with the Node.js sidecar running the SDK.

| `// Jaeger Go Backend import "github.com/a2aproject/a2a-go-sdk" func (s *QueryServer) AnalyzeTrace(ctx context.Context, traceID string) {     // Connect to the agnostic A2A endpoint     client := a2a.NewClient("http://agent-sidecar:9000")          // The A2A protocol handles "Tasks" and "Messages" generically     task, _ := client.CreateTask("Analyze this trace", traceID)          for update := range task.StreamUpdates() {         // Forward standardized A2A events         // (thought, tool_use, artifact) to Jaeger UI         s.StreamToUI(update)     } }` |
| :---- |

#### 

#### **Step C: The "Agentic Loop" (In Node.js)**

The sidecar runs the logic you mentioned earlier:

| `// Inside the Node.js sidecarimport { ClaudeAgent } from '@anthropic-ai/agent-sdk';const agent = new ClaudeAgent({ mcpServers: [YOUR_JAEGER_MCP] });const stream = await agent.query(userInput);// For each thought/action, the sidecar sends an A2A message back to Gofor await (const message of stream) {    sendToGoBackend(message); }` |
| :---- |

##### **Existing Facades (The "Wrappers")**

There are three main frameworks that provide this "Common A2A API" today:

* Microsoft Agent Framework (`A2AAgent`): A library that allows you to treat any remote URL as a local agent object. It resolves the `AgentCard` automatically, so you just call `agent.execute()`.  
* Google Agent Engine: A managed "Hub" where you can register any A2A-compliant agent. It provides a single API to talk to "Vertex AI Agents," "CrewAI Agents," or your custom "Jaeger sidecars."

#### **Step D: Driving the UI (AG-UI)**

The Go backend takes those messages and wraps them in **AG-UI events**.

* If CC says: I'm calling get\_trace(882f), the UI shows a "Fetching Trace..." spinner.  
* If CC says: highlight\_span("db-query-1"), the React UI (via a useAgent hook) automatically scrolls the Gantt chart to that span and highlights it in red.

### ---

**4\. Why this works for "Bringing Your Own Agent"**

If tomorrow you want to switch to **Gemini-cli**:

1. You don't touch the React UI.  
2. You don't touch the Go backend.  
3. You simply swap the **Node.js sidecar** for one that uses the **Google ADK**.  
   As long as the sidecar speaks the **A2A protocol**, the rest of the system is oblivious to which "Brain" is doing the work.

### **Summary**

To build this, your immediate "to-do" list is:

1. **Define the Agent Card** in your Go backend.  
2. **Create a Node.js "Agent Runner"** sidecar that wraps the Claude Agent SDK and exposes an A2A-compliant endpoint.  
3. **Add an AG-UI client** to your React frontend (CopilotKit is the easiest shortcut for this).  
4. 

### **Final Architecture**

**To get CC into your Jaeger UI, here is the minimal "Industrial Grade" setup:**

| Layer | Component | Role |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **User Interface** | **React \+ AG-UI** | **Listens for A2A-standardized messages (e.g. highlight\_span) to drive the UI.** |
| **Control Plane** | **Go A2A Client** | **Manages the "Task" lifecycle. If you want to switch to Gemini, you just point this URL to a different sidecar.import "https://github.com/a2aproject/a2a-go"**  |
| **The "Translator"** | **ADK / MS Framework** | **This is your Facade. It wraps the proprietary Claude Agent SDK and exposes the /.well-known/agent-card.json.** |
| **The Intelligence** | **Claude Agent SDK** | **The actual "CC" engine doing the work.** |

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>
